heading: Relationship between the Inter-American Convention and the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards ofJune 10, 1958
label: 305 Relationship between the Inter-American Convention and the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards ofJune 10, 1958
notes: Effective DateSection effective upon the entry into force of the Inter-American Convention on International Commercial Arbitration of , with respect to the United States (), see , set out as a note under .January 30, 1975Oct. 27, 1990section 3 of Pub. L. 101–369section 301 of this title

children.text: If a majority of the parties to the arbitration agreement are citizens of a State or States that have ratified or acceded to the Inter-American Convention and are member States of the Organization of American States, the Inter-American Convention shall apply.
In all other cases the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards of , shall apply.June 10, 1958
